text: 13g of fat. omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. the rest is not doing much. in this category, carbs barely matter.
20.4g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 117% daily value, vitamin d at 66% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
minerals are basically not adding much here.

on the plus side: easy to build a strong meal around. it works as a genuine centrepiece instead of needing lots of nutritional excuses. very satisfying as a protein source. that makes it practical in real meals. has unusually strong trust with viewers. people already read it as a serious healthy-food choice, which matches its overall case.

now the downsides: can be expensive. price can make it less practical. quality varies with sourcing. sourcing changes the real-world quality. needs good storage and handling. it is less convenient than shelf-stable options.
S tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
